other_observers: Mark Peterson, Bill Maynard, Brandon Percival in the am and Norm Erthal and Steve Stohoviak sp in the pm; Brian Gibbons
duration: half hour in the am and half hour in the afternoon
description: see photos. One deceptive artifact of the pics are that the eye-ring looks more almond-shaped than it was in life after being released. When seen from a distance in the scope the eye-ring was a touch elongated but was even around the eye.Behaviors: Once released the bird flew up into cottonwoods and disappeared up high. Later in the afternoon it was refound foraging low in Russian Olives perching from 2-20 feet up and feeding often.Call: upslurred saweeep or sweeepPlumage: adult
